fact: July 24 is exactly 205 days into the year, which means there are exactly 160 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is peak summer heat season - the sun blazes down for about 15 hours a day in many places!
Cicadas are typically screaming their loudest around this time of year in North America, making that crazy buzzing sound that can reach 120 decibels - louder than a chainsaw!
July 24 falls in the middle of Leo season in the zodiac, when the lion constellation is prominent in summer skies.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this date is deep winter - penguins are huddling together while the north is sweating!
Fireflies are usually putting on their best light shows right around this time in temperate regions, blinking in patterns to find their mates in the dark.
The sun rises super early on July 24 - in northern places like Alaska, it barely sets at all and stays bright almost all night long!
This date is roughly 33 days after the summer solstice, which means daylight is already starting to shrink by about 2 minutes per day in northern climates.
Corn plants are typically growing like crazy around this time in farming regions - they can actually grow up to 4 inches in a single day when the weather is hot and humid!
July 24 is often prime thunderstorm season in many temperate areas - the hot air creates perfect conditions for those massive, rumbling storm clouds.
Bats are usually having a feast right now because there are tons of insects flying around in the warm summer air - a single bat can gobble up 1,000 mosquitoes in one hour!
Ocean water often reaches some of its warmest temperatures around this date in northern coastal areas, making it perfect for sea creatures like jellyfish to multiply.
